body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: /critic and scripts/critic.py produce actionable critique
body.intention: I intend that /critic and scripts/critic.py are useful before wiring them into automated decision flows.
body.description:
**What changed:** /critic skill and scripts/critic.py available for adversarial analysis of code, decisions, tickets, and text.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- Critic prompt is too generic, produces boilerplate ("use error handling, add tests, improve logging")
- Critic finds fake issues (flags things that are correct but unfamiliar)
- Critic misses obvious issues because context is incomplete
- CC spends time on critique output that isn't actionable
- Critic could hallucinate file paths or function names not in codebase

**Signals to watch:**
- Spot-check 5 recent /critic or scripts/critic.py runs: are outputs useful? Do they flag real issues?
- Ticket feedback: are critiques acted on, or ignored as boilerplate?
- Prompt iteration: if early runs are too generic, refine prompt and re-test
- No false positives (flagged issues that turn out to be correct on inspection)

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-22 (7 days) to refine prompt if needed; gate at 2026-06-29 for final observation.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe prediction effects; annotate actionability of critiques; recommend prompt refinements if needed
**Test plan:** no tests — this is an observation ticket
